dialogos.texto_en: Where was it, Yotsuba-san?!
It was around here! I definitely saw it!
...What are you guys doing?
Ah, Uesugi-san!
Raiha was with you, huh.\nHave you finished talking?
Yes, we're finished.
So, what are you doing?\nDid you see something in the ocean?
I saw a whale spout earlier!
What? A whale?
I missed it. Can you do it one more time, whale-san?
Err, I think you might've imagined things.
I didn't imagine anything!\nWater just shot up from the surface,\nlike fwooosh!
But...it only happened once...\nso the whale might have gone somewhere else.
I wanted to see it, too.
Hey, big bro.\nWhat kind of whale was it,\nif it could be seen from here?
I'm not sure, but maybe it was a humpback whale?\nThey come to the ocean off Okinawa\nto raise their young during this season.
What?! So can we see baby whales too?
Go whale watching out to sea,\nand there's a chance you can see parent and child.
I wanna see that!\nI want to see parent and child whales spout together!
That would be nice. They would probably be so cute,\nside-by-side, spouting water up in the air.
But, why DO whales spout water like that?
You seemed so interested\nin whales a moment ago.\nDon't you know?
It's not a problem if I don't know, right?\nI can still be impressed.
That's not something to brag about.\nFirst, try thinking about it for a minute.
Hm...
Is it a greeting?\nLike saying hello?
So, you're saying it's directed towards us?
Yes, that's it.
So listen, whales don't need to communicate with humans\nto live their lives.
Ah! That's right!
And, the answer to why they spout water\nis because of their breathing.
Breathing, huh?
Raiha, what about you? Do you know?
Ehehe, actually, I do.
Ohh, nice going, Raiha-chan!
You like to look at encyclopedias, don't you, Raiha.
Yeah, I often look at the field guide of sea creatures.
Wowww, I respect you even more now!\nSo, being studious runs in the blood\nof the Uesugi family, huh?
(Yeah, but Dad... I wonder about him, though.\n He used to say he was a hardcore outlaw.)
And, the answer to why they spout water\nis because of their breathing.
Breathing, huh?
Well, that's an interesting idea.\nUsing spouting for communication.
Ehehe, it's not that interesting, really.
But who are they communicating with?\nOther whales swim underwater, you know?
Oh, right! It won't work if it's not visible.
Ah, that's why whales use ultrasound...\nIt's called echolocation,\nand they use it to communicate with their companions.
I see, that sounds like an earth-friendly method!
That's how ecology works.\nDon't judge things just based on how they sound.
And, the answer to why they spout water\nis because of their breathing.
Breathing, huh?
Unlike fish, whales breathe through their lungs.\nIn other words, they can't breathe in water.
Ah!\nThat's right! Whales and dolphins are mammals!
Exactly.\nIt's the same as when humans swim and take a breath.
The breath that's forcefully exhaled\nlooks like water spray or white mist.\nThat's the true nature of spouting.
I knew they were taking a breath,\nbut why does it look like a fountain?
That's a good question!\nIt's a reaction caused by temperature difference.
The air that was warmed by the whale's body temperature\nlooks white when it touches the cold air outside.
We also see our breath as white on a cold day,\ndon't we?
Oh, wow! So that's the same thing!
I see.\nI completely thought they were spitting out seawater.
That's a common misunderstanding.\nBut, I can understand why it might look like that.
Hehehe, I learned something new again!
Anyway, Yotsuba.\nIsn't it time for everyone to meet up?
Oh, right!\nI lost track of time during the whale lecture!
Let's summarize what we heard from the others\nand decide what to do.
Okay!
